---
title: "Chapter 10: The Experimentation Evaluation Approach to Outcome Evaluation"
description: "Detailed study notes for Chapter 10 of Practical Program Evaluation."
book_id: "practical-program-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Chapter"
chapter_number: 10
source_pages: "268-280"
citation_key: "chen_2015_practical_program_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:practical-program-evaluation:ch10-the-experimentation-evaluation-approach-to-outcome-evaluation:c0001"
  - "chunk:practical-program-evaluation:ch10-research-designs-for-ruling-out-threats-to-internal-validity:c0001"
  - "chunk:practical-program-evaluation:ch10-questions-for-reflection:c0001"
  - "chunk:practical-program-evaluation:ch02-outcome-stage:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---

## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2015</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Chapter 10</span><span>Source pages 268-280</span></div>

This note summarises Chapter 10, "The Experimentation Evaluation Approach to Outcome Evaluation," from Huey T. Chen's *Practical Program Evaluation* [@chen_2015_practical_program_evaluation]. It is a paraphrased study note based on source-linked MCP research-library retrieval records, not a substitute for the chapter.

The retrieval trail is retained in the source records at the bottom of the page. The note paraphrases the chapter's argument and procedures, and it does not reproduce textbook tables, review questions, checklists, exhibits, or extended passages. When a table or figure is central, the note describes its function and leaves the source artifact in the evidence record.

## Core Argument

The experimentation approach prioritizes internal validity and pure independent effects, making it valuable for some conclusive outcome questions but incomplete for many real-world evaluation needs.

## Study Summary

Chapter 10 presents the dominant conclusive outcome tradition: validity-focused outcome evaluation guided by experimentation logic.

The approach asks whether an intervention caused intended outcomes by ruling out threats to internal validity. It therefore values research designs that support credible causal inference, especially experimental and strong quasi-experimental designs.

Chen treats the approach seriously but conditionally. It is appropriate when stakeholders need rigorous evidence about whether an intervention affected outcomes apart from confounding factors.

The chapter also prepares the critique developed in Chapter 11. A design that maximizes internal validity may bracket the real-world supports and adaptations that stakeholders actually use to make interventions work.

For close reading, track how the chapter uses validity-focused outcome evaluation, experimentation evaluation approach, internal validity, and pure independent effects while moving through experimentation approach, Campbellian validity logic, and threats to internal validity. The notes below identify what the chapter asks the evaluator to notice, what design problem it helps solve, and what should be checked against the saved evidence records before relying on the summary in applied work.

Within the wider book, this chapter belongs to the outcome sequence: it moves from readiness for outcome evaluation to experimentation, real-world effectuality, and theory-driven explanation.

## Key Concepts

- **validity-focused outcome evaluation**: Outcome evaluation guided by experimentation logic and the priority of ruling out threats to internal validity.
- **experimentation evaluation approach**: Outcome evaluation that prioritizes experimental or quasi-experimental logic to estimate pure causal effects.
- **internal validity**: The credibility of a causal claim that observed outcomes were produced by the intervention rather than confounding influences.
- **pure independent effects**: Effects attributed to an intervention after removing or controlling for other influences and supports.
- **randomized experiment**: A causal design that uses random assignment to strengthen claims about intervention effects.
- **threats to validity**: Alternative explanations, biases, or design problems that can weaken causal or evaluative claims.
- **conclusive outcome evaluation**: Outcome evaluation used to judge whether an intervention achieved intended effects or merit.

## Chapter Structure

- Experimentation approach.
- Campbellian validity logic.
- Threats to internal validity.
- Research designs.
- Strengths.
- Limits.

## Practical Implications

- Use experimentation logic when pure causal attribution is the central question.
- Make validity threats explicit in outcome design.
- Do not assume internal-validity priority answers every stakeholder question.

## Concept Map

::: {.concept-map}
```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  Problem["Problem: What does Chapter 10 help decide?"]
  Central["Concept: The Experimentation Evaluation Approach to Outcome Evaluation"]
  Concept1["Concept: validity-focused outcome evaluation"]
  Concept2["Concept: experimentation evaluation approach"]
  Concept3["Concept: internal validity"]
  Mechanism1["Mechanism: experimentation approach"]
  Mechanism2["Mechanism: Campbellian validity logic"]
  Mechanism3["Mechanism: threats to internal validity"]
  Implication1["Practice implication: Use experimentation logic when pure causal attribution is the central question."]
  Implication2["Practice implication: Make validity threats explicit in outcome design."]
  Question["Open question: What evidence would make this usable in a live evaluation?"]

  Problem --> Central
  Central --> Question
  Central --> Concept1
  Central --> Concept2
  Central --> Concept3
  Concept1 --> Mechanism1
  Concept2 --> Mechanism2
  Concept3 --> Mechanism3
  Mechanism1 --> Implication1
  Mechanism2 --> Implication2
  Implication1 --> Question

  classDef problem fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#b91c1c,color:#7f1d1d;
  classDef concept fill:#e6f3f1,stroke:#115e59,color:#134e4a;
  classDef mechanism fill:#e8f1ff,stroke:#1d4ed8,color:#1e3a8a;
  classDef implication fill:#fce7f3,stroke:#be185d,color:#831843;
  class Problem problem;
  class Central concept;
  class Concept1,Concept2,Concept3 concept;
  class Mechanism1,Mechanism2,Mechanism3 mechanism;
  class Implication1,Implication2,Question implication;
```
:::

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Reading route: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Concepts synthesis: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)
- Previous: [Chapter 9: Constructive Outcome Evaluations](chapter-09-constructive-outcome-evaluations.qmd)
- Next: [Chapter 11: The Holistic Effectuality Evaluation Approach to Outcome Evaluation](chapter-11-the-holistic-effectuality-evaluation-approach-to-outcome-evaluation.qmd)

## Study Prompts

1. What evaluation design decision does Chapter 10 help make?
2. Which stakeholder assumptions would need evidence before applying this guidance?
3. How would this chapter change scoping, data collection, interpretation, or use?
4. Where could this guidance be misused if treated as a fixed template?

## Source Records

<details class="source-chunks">
<summary>Source records</summary>
<ul class="source-chunk-list">
<li><code>chunk:practical-program-evaluation:ch10-the-experimentation-evaluation-approach-to-outcome-evaluation:c0001</code></li>
<li><code>chunk:practical-program-evaluation:ch10-research-designs-for-ruling-out-threats-to-internal-validity:c0001</code></li>
<li><code>chunk:practical-program-evaluation:ch10-questions-for-reflection:c0001</code></li>
<li><code>chunk:practical-program-evaluation:ch02-outcome-stage:c0001</code></li>
</ul>
</details>

## References

- Chen, H. T. (2015). *Practical Program Evaluation*. SAGE Publications. Chapter 10, pages 268-280.
- Saved retrieval metadata: `data/research-library/evidence/practical-program-evaluation/chapter-evidence-summary.json`.
